records.ety: [F. poursuite, fr. poursuivre. See Pursue, v. t.]

defs: The act of following or going after; esp., a following with haste, either for sport or in hostility; chase; prosecution; as, the pursuit of game; the pursuit of an enemy.
quotes: Weak we are, and can not shun pursuit.

defs: A following with a view to reach, accomplish, or obtain; endeavor to attain to or gain; as, the pursuit of knowledge; the pursuit of happiness or pleasure.
Course of business or occupation; continued employment with a view to same end; as, mercantile pursuits; a literary pursuit.

quotes: That pursuit for tithes ought, and of ancient time did pertain to the spiritual court.
